The Conciergerie’s four towers are located on the northern side.

Tour Cesar and Tour d’Argent The oldest, the Tour Bonbec, was built in 1250 and initially served as a prison. The Conciergerie, as we know it today, didn’t yet exist. This tower has retained its original crenelations and its pepper-pot roof was added in the 16th century. Its name emerged in the 15th century when it housed the Salle de la Question, the torture room. In Old French, “bec” meant mouth, hence its nickname Bon Bec, as the unfortunate prisoners often ended up confessing what was expected of  ... Another curiosity of the Conciergerie is the gothic room of the Gens D’Armes which housed the King’s soldiers of the guard. Remarkably well preserved, it evokes perfectly barrack life at the time of its construction by Philippe the Good during the 14th century.
